sourceMovie.title: The Gift
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Gift (2015) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dream House
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Gift clicked for you, Dream House (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Inland Empire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Gift clicked for you, Inland Empire (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Gift?
faq.answer: Try Dream House, Inland Empire, Gaslight—each shares DNA with The Gift while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Gift?
faq.answer: The Gift (2015) is best described as Thriller, Mystery, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Gift worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
